title: Fixing Broken HTML Image Tags with Missing 'alt' Attributes

statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function `fixHtmlImageTags` that takes a string of HTML content as input and returns the modified string with all image tags (``) having a valid `alt` attribute if it's missing. The `alt` attribute should be set to the image's `src` attribute. Assume that the input HTML string is well-formed and contains only one `img` tag per line. If an image tag has multiple `alt` attributes, remove all of them.
statement.notes: Preserve the original order and structure of the HTML content. Don't remove or add any tags other than `img`.

examples.explanation: The function adds the `alt` attribute to the first `img` tag.
The function adds the `alt` attribute to the first `img` tag.

hints.text: Use regular expressions to find and modify the image tags.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Broken HTML Image Tags with Missing 'alt' Attributes
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate over each line in the HTML content and check if the `img` tag has an `alt` attribute.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a brute-force approach to find and replace each `img` tag.
editorial.betterSolution: Use regular expressions to find and modify the `img` tags in a single pass.
editorial.optimalSolution: Regular expressions can efficiently find and replace the `img` tags in a single pass, reducing the time complexity.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The modified HTML string will have all `img` tags with a valid `alt` attribute if it's missing.
editorial.dryRun: Test the function with the provided examples to ensure correctness.
